mnemonics.title: ACID Properties
mnemonics.content:
Atomicity: All or Nothing
Consistency: Valid State
Isolation: Concurrent like Serial
Durability: Permanent Once Committed

mnemonics.title: 2PL Phases
mnemonics.content:
GROWing = Get locks, Releasing = SHRINKing
Remember: First GROW then SHRINK, never back from SHRINK to GROW

mnemonics.title: Lock Types
mnemonics.content:
S = Share (Several can read)
X = Xclude (Xclusive - one at a time)
S + S = OK | S + X = No | X + X = No

acronyms.usage: A concurrency control protocol with growing and shrinking phases
A protocol for ensuring atomic commit in distributed databases
Lock allowing concurrent reads, also called Read Lock
Lock allowing exclusive read/write, also called Write Lock
Timestamp of the last transaction that successfully read a data item
Timestamp of the last transaction that successfully wrote a data item

visualTips.visualization: Imagine two people editing a Google Doc simultaneously. The last person's changes overwrite the first person's changes without saving the first version.
Like reading a draft email that gets recalled/deleted before being sent. You read something that no longer exists.
Think of a balloon: it first inflates (growing phase = acquiring locks), then deflates (shrinking phase = releasing locks). You can't reinflate once you start deflating.
Think of a flow chart where arrows show which transaction preceded which. If you can follow arrows in a loop back to start, you have a cycle = not serializable.
Two people each holding one item and waiting for the other person's item. Neither can proceed without the other letting go.

analogies.analogy: Like traffic signals at an intersection - controlling multiple cars (transactions) to prevent collisions (inconsistencies) while allowing flow (parallel execution).
Multiple people can read a sign (shared view) but only one person can paint over it (exclusive write).
Like a exam system: students (transactions) take the test independently (execution phase), then papers are checked for cheating (validation phase), and only honest ones pass.
Like a food expiration system - older timestamps have priority. A new dish can't overwrite a newer dish's spot in the refrigerator.
Like only reading published books (committed data), not reading a book someone is still writing (uncommitted data) that might be discarded.
